title: Annual safe-withdrawal-rate review — re-underwrite the 3.6% plan
description: The yearly re-underwrite of the glidepath: trailing spend, portfolio value at the June 30 mark, and the cash-runway refill rule all get re-checked before the August transfer is set. The line to hold is 3.6% — sequence risk in the early years is the whole game. Children carry the mechanical steps.

title: Recompute trailing-12-month spend from the account exports
description: Pull the card and checking exports, categorize, and post the trailing-12 number. The spend figure is the denominator of the whole review — nothing downstream moves until it lands.
close_reason: Trailing-12-month spend posted at $86,400 — roughly 3.5% of the June 30 portfolio value, inside the 3.6% line with a little margin. Category detail filed with the review note.

comments.author: steward
comments.text: Caught one anomaly before posting: the cruise deposit appeared in the travel category twice (card and checking sides of the same payment). Deduplicated; the corrected total is what shipped.

title: Model the withdrawal rate against the cash-runway refill rule
description: The high-yield savings runway covers ~20 months of spend. Question for the model: refill it from dividends as they land, or from a quarterly sale? Run both against the posted spend number and pick the rule for the next 12 months.

title: Set the August withdrawal transfer + top up the checking buffer
description: Once the refill rule is chosen, set the August transfer from the runway to checking and schedule it. Blocked on the model — the transfer size is its output, not an input.

title: Roth conversion ladder — size and queue the 2026 tranche
description: The bridge-years play: convert Rollover-IRA dollars to Roth while income is low, filling the low brackets before Social Security starts. The constraint is not the bracket — it's the marketplace-subsidy math, since every converted dollar is MAGI. Execution window is the December review on the tax calendar.

title: Project 2026 taxable income before sizing the conversion
description: Dividends from the income sleeve plus the realized gain from the Q2 IBM tranche are the base; the conversion sits on top. Project the full-year number first.
close_reason: Projection posted: sleeve dividends plus the Q2 IBM tranche gain put full-year income at ~$74k before any conversion. There is real headroom, but the subsidy math (glide-c3) decides how much of it is actually usable.

comments.author: steward
comments.text: Cross-checked the IBM gain against the June execution ticket — the 2020 lot, long-term treatment confirmed. The 2024 loss lot stays untouched as harvest inventory.

title: Pin down the marketplace-subsidy cliff before sizing the tranche
description: Pre-65 health coverage runs through the marketplace, and the premium subsidy keys off MAGI — a conversion sized past the cliff can make the marginal converted dollar startlingly expensive. Model the premium cost at three conversion sizes and mark where the effective rate turns.

title: Queue the first Rollover-IRA → Roth conversion tranche
description: Paperwork and the in-kind vs cash decision, staged for the December window. Blocked on the subsidy-cliff model — the tranche size is its output.

title: Mediterranean cruise — lock the slow-travel bookends
description: The 14-night Barcelona → Rome → Athens sailing (Oct 8–22) is booked; what's open is the time-rich part — a Barcelona week before embarkation and an Athens week after, plus the shore plan. Slow travel is the point of being retired.

title: Hold the Barcelona apartment for the pre-cruise week
description: A week in the city before embarkation beats a hotel night and a rushed morning. Wants: walkable old town, a real kitchen, free cancellation deep enough to cover an itinerary change.
close_reason: Held a two-bedroom in the Gothic Quarter for Oct 1–8, free cancellation through Sep 1. Embarkation-day logistics reduce to a 15-minute taxi to the port.

comments.author: scout
comments.text: Cancellation deadline logged for Sep 1 — same date the outbound PHX award hold expires, so both decisions land together.

title: Shortlist shore excursions for the three anchor ports
description: Food + antiquity, per the trip charter. Independent plans where the port sits close to town; ship excursions only where the tender schedule makes independent timing risky. Deliverable is a one-page shortlist per anchor port.

title: Book the Athens post-cruise week + the PHX return
description: Disembark Oct 22, then a week in Athens before flying home — decide 5 vs 7 nights, then ticket the one-way return. Blocked on the Barcelona hold: the land bookends were priced as a pair, and the return fare class keys off the final trip length.

title: Q3 IBM unwind tranche — pick the lot and the window
description: The Q2 tranche sold the 2020 lot into the bond sleeve per the June ticket; the career-accumulated IBM position is the last real single-name concentration. Q3 candidate is a slice of the 2016 lot — long-term gain, measured size — while the 2024 loss lot stays parked as harvest inventory.

title: Sweep the June dividend cash into the runway
description: Quarter-end distributions from the dividend sleeve and the bond fund are sitting in the settlement account — move them to the high-yield savings before they drift into spending.
close_reason: June distributions from SCHD, VYM, the single names, and BND swept to the high-yield savings. The runway sits restocked ahead of the August transfer decision.

title: Compare O vs VICI for the income-wishlist slot
description: Both sit on the wishlist; at most one earns a starter position. Payout coverage, lease structure, and rate sensitivity — an hour with the filings, no rush.

title: Review marketplace health-plan options ahead of open enrollment
description: The annual pre-65 ritual: re-shop the plan before the November window. Parked until the fall plan documents publish — nothing to compare against yet.

title: Refresh the Cascade Robotics advisory notes before the fall board cycle
description: The light advisory watch, not a job hunt: re-read the last two quarters of public updates and refresh the notes so a fall conversation — if one comes — starts warm.
